Question: Can the magnitude of $$L_{z}$$ ever be equal to L ?
Answer: No. The quantum number $$m = \text{unicode[:-]} l , \text{unicode[:-]} l + 1 \text{,unicode[:ddot.]} , 0 \text{,unicode[:ddot.]} , l - 1 , l .$$ Thus, the magnitude of $$L_{z}$$ is always less than L because $$< \sqrt{l ( l + 1 )}$$
